14
Handwritten notes in PKD’s hand: A Light struck meadow for Tony Hiss & the Real World. Hark! Each tree its silence breaks – Nicholas Brody 1692

When I met Theodore Surgeon, who wrote MORE THAN HUMAN, this good man said to me right off, “What sort of universe is it that causes a man like Tony Boucher to die of cancer?” I had been wondering the same thing ever since Tony Boucher died. So had Ted Sturgeon, although he didn’t expect me to give an answer. He just wanted to show me what he – Ted Sturgeon – was like. I’ve found I can do that, too; let people know about me by asking that. It shows that I cared a lot about one of the warmest people who ever lived. Tony was warm and at the same time when he stood in the midst of a group of people, seat came out of his forehead from fear. Nobody ever wrote that about him but it’s true. He was terrified all the time. He told me so once, not in so many words. He loved people, but one time I met him on the electric train going to be opera and he was scared. He was music critic and he did reviewing for the New York Times and edited a magazine and wrote novels and stories. But he was scared to take a drive across town.

Tony loved the universe and the universe frightened him, and I think I know where his head was at. A lot of people who are timid are that way because they love too much. They’re afraid it’ll all fall through. Naturally, it did with Tony. He died in middle-age. Now, I ask you, what good did it do him to be scared? He used to carry his rare old 78 records to radio station KPFA every week wrapping them up in a towel so they wouldn’t get broken. One time I decided to give Tony all my rare opera and vocal records, just plain give plain give them to him as a gift of my loving him. I phoned him up. “got Tisne Lemnitz records and Gerhard Husch,” I told him. Tony replied shyly, “They are my idols.” We was a Roman Catholic – the only one we knew – and that was a strong statement. Before I could get the records to him he was dead. “I feel tired half the day,” he had added. “I can’t work as much as I used to. I think I’m sick.” I explained I had the same thing. That was eight or so years ago. The doctor told him he had a bruised rib and taped it up. Someday I will meet that doctor on the street. Tony got bad advice from everyone who could talk.

We used to play poker. Tony loved opera and poker and science fiction and mystery stories. He had a little writing class – this was after he was famous and edited F&SF – and he charged on dollar a night when you showed up. He read your whole manuscript. He told you how rotten it was, and you sent away and wrote something good. I never figured out how he accomplished that. Criticisim like that is supposed to crush you. “Maybe it’s because when Tony reads your story it’s like he’s reading it in Latin,” somebody said. A whole dollar it cost. He taught me to write, and my first sale was to him. I still can remember that nobody understood the story but him, even after it was printed. Now it’s in a college-level s-f course manual put out by Ginn and Company. There’s only about 300 words to the story. After the printing of the story, Ginn and Company prints an impromptu discussion I had with a high school class about the story. All the kids understand the story. It’s about how a dog sees garbagemen coming to steal the precious food that the family stores up every day until the heavily constructed repository is full and then these Roogs come and steal the

Last edit over 5 years ago by Zebrapedia
15
Indexed
15
food just when it’s ripe and perfect. The dog tries to warn the family, but it’s always early in the morning and his barking just annoys them. The story ends when the family decides they have to get rid of the dog, due to his barking, at which point one of the Roogs or garbagemen says to the dog, “We’ll be back to get the people pretty soon.” I never could understand why no one but Tony Boucher could understand the story (I sent it to him in 1951). I guess in those days my view of garbagemen was not shared universally, and now in 1971 when the high school class discussed it with me, I guess it is. “But garbagemen don’t eat people,” a lady editor pointed out to me in 1952. I had trouble answering that. Something comes and eats up people who are sleeping in tranquility. Like Tony. Something got him. I think the dog, who cried “Roog. Roog,” was trying to warn me and Tony. I got the warning and escaped – for a while, anyhow – but Tony stayed at his post. You see, when you’re so scared of the universe (or Roogs, if you will) to stay at your post takes courage the kind they can’t write about because (one) they don’t know how and (two) they didn’t notice in the first place, except maybe Ted Sturgeon, with all his own love, and his total lack of fear. He must have known how scared Tony was, and to be that scared and for the Roogs to get you – it’s so symmetric, isn’t it.

However, Tony is still alive, I discovered last year. My cat had begun to behave in an odd way, keeping watch over me in a quiet fashion, and I saw that he had changed. This was after he ran away and came back, wild and dirty, crapping on the rug in fear; we took him to the vet and the vet calmed him down and healed him. After that Pinky had what I call a spiritual quality, except that he wouldn’t eat meat. He would tremble whenever we tried to feed it to him. For five months he’d been lost, living in the gutter, seeing god knows what; I wish I knew. Anyhow when he was changed – in the twinkling of an eye; that is, while at the vet – he wouldn’t ever do anything cruel. Yet I knew Pinky was afraid, because once I almost shut the refrigerator door on him and he did a 3 cushion bank shot of himself off the walls to escape, and clocked a velocity unusual for a pink sheep thing that usually just sat and gazed ahead. Pinky had trouble breathing because of his heavy fur and what they call hairballs. Tony had asthma terribly and needed it cold. Pinky would sit by the door to get the cold air from under the crack, and struggle to breathe. I will not write a teaser article here; Pinky died of cancer suddenly; he was three years old; very young for a cat. It was totally unexpected. The vet diagnosed it as something else.

I hadn’t realized Pinky was Tony Boucher, served up by the universe again, until I had this dream about Tony the Tiger – the cereal box character who offers you cocoa puffs. In my dream I stood at the one end of a light-struck glade, and at the other a great tiger came out slowly, with delight, and I knew we were together again, Tony the Tiger and me. My joy was unbounded. When I woke up I tried to think who I knew named Tony. I had other strange experiences after Pink died. I dreamed about “Mrs. Donlevy” who was incredibly tall – I could see only her feet and ankles – and she was serving me a place of milk on the back porch ? there was a vacant lot where I could roam at will, forever. It was ? Elysian Vacant Lot, which the Greeks believed in, but just my size.

Last edit over 5 years ago by Harpax
72
Needs Review
72
between two opposite sequential states; my experience began when I was "listening for very weak signals to transduce," which was meekness (I got it right, picked up paw talk, etc), and then came the chromatic fire. That was when I received God or God's Power or Spirit, the Yang upon me-as-Yin. Possession of me by the God took place, as Ted Sturgeon says in VENUS PLUS X or whatever, this being what was really sought at the Feast of Agape. No wonder they say, as Doris points our, God is love! Wow, He sure is! It is a (ahem) mating (again vide Yeat's poem "Leda and the Swan.") But that was one year ago -- over a year ago. Actual possession lasted days, weeks, slowly drained off; no more than a couple weeks, the electrostatic life form gradually drained off. But Firebright remained; the dreams remain right up to now; contact (Fellowship) with God remains.

Spring is the mating season. As Psy Today says, it's based all the way up to cosmic influences (sidereal). All synchronized.

By following all the admonitions of the Gospels, one literally courted the great masculine Father deity so that he literally possessed the Christian. From this (receiving the Holy Spirit, as they put it euph[e]mistically) they got various powers: healing, prophecy, ability to discern, and were made Righteous, which I experienced as a thirst for Truth and Justice and going the right thing. "Gifts of the Spirit," yes, but those who were possesses were also the "First fruits of the harvest," which meant that they gained something permanent; this would correspond with my being back in Rome; i.e. escaping the thrall producing death -- hence, freedom, release, from the power of death. Certainly, of all the various gifts, this would matter the most. I myself experienced reality on an inner ontological basis (assimilating objects themselves rather than mere phenomena), saw the structure of the universe (Logos or Plan), have and still retain fellowship --contact-- with God, which is to say, knowledge of him. I didn't just get the power to heal; I was healed. I walked with God and communed with him (along the alley that day, also in trances and dreams). I knew the true state of things (the tyranny) and what to do. But to dream of Crete or dream of Rome is a lot difference than that perios when I was there; when for me time was abolished. This is what I mean when I say, The power of the world over me was broken. It got that cardboard fake quality. Now it is clear about the Father-Son (JHWH-Christ) relatinship (Zeus-Zagreus). The Holy Spirit, a form God the Father takes, impregnates (the person receives it) and Christ consciousness is born (in Mary's case, Jesus Christ physically born from her womb, she being made with child by the Holy Spirit). Out of this union comes Christ. The father is God. The impregnating spirit is the Holy Spirit. (Does Christ grow up to be Jehovah? Why hasn't anyone ever asked that before? Or like him? I think he can be like him or even be him.) Where is the Logos in all this? The outcome of possession by the deity is Logos relatedness, in the sense Hericlitus meant. One (the receiving one) is brought into conformity with it, while the Christ within, Firebright, grows. The former mere moral human, full of sin (sickness and insanity) is consumed by the growing Christ, moves toward him, toward the Logos, with it as exemplar and outcome: as perfected form. The Logos is the plan and information which leads to that completion, a process begun by the Holy Spirit initially.

Needs Review
169
I note that Jung says that the AMORC rose represents Dionysos. Since it is placed on the cross, where the Savior was, I assume that if Jung is correct then the rose placed dead-center on the cross is a symbol telling us that our Savior is Dionysos, or, conversely, that Dionysos died on the cross for us, in which case he is our Savior, both of which come out the same extraordinary thing. Also, I had begun to have dreams/visions suggesting Dionysos or at least Zagreus was in fact Jesus, was in fact our dying and then reborn God whom we worship; this is another basic secret disclosed to me. Then, too, the EB speaks of Dionysos as the "god of fruitfulness," which is "felix" once more, and related to Genesis: "Go forth and be fruitful." He is a harvest or grain god. The furrow is the grave; also it is the sexual act -- the phallus is buried in the ditch, and then later there is a harvest. A mystery is revealed here, enacted at Eleuysius. But the experience of the god --being possess-- that is the communion experience, the central matter, as shown in the BACCHAE. The world has never been ready for the god of fruitfulness, which seems twisted. Is life such a frightening thing? In Rollo May's terms, he is Eros versus Thanatos. Who is for thanatos and not for eros? Many people -- the state, to name one. The state crushes life. (Vide the BACCHAE, in which King Pentheus, sorrow, wishes to destroy the stranger, who brings life. It is an archetypal story, with life always winning eventually, having a wild and unpredictable power. The state always goes on formula. It builds prisons and winds up in them, victim of its own slogans. As in EYE IN THE SKY, the slogans fall on those who use (which is misuse) them. The key quality of our god is spontaneity. You can never tell what he is going to say or do next, or even where he will next appear, or most extraordinary of all, what he will look like. Perhaps the next person you meet will be him.

. . . The Meister Eckehart says is absolutely crucial to me, to the highest extent, because: (1) from the beginning I have felt that my 3 74 experience was possession of me by god; (2) that Christianity was involved; (3) which means that Christianity secretly has had to do, always, with being possessed by god, like other mystery religions (which is what Ted Sturgeon says in his novel about Venus). It is all tied together by what Meister Eckehart says about God being born is your soul which is the actual Kingdom of God. Everything else is secondary to this, and probably subsidiary, such as the anamnesis, etc. Eckehart confirms this.

. . . The Eb's article on "Monotheism" says: "Monotheism presumes two entities: God and the universe. But God's relationship to the universe is not merely that of prime mover. He still approaches it, and the manifestations of his continual activity toward it are expressed in history," I am paraphrasing; I guess it means in historical events and certain trends or directions which the flow of history takes. It doesn't have to be all history, but (1) He does manifest himself to us still and (2) we find that in what we call "history" and (3) certain historical events can be said to be manifestations of God or from God. This does not mean that we can discern which events are from him, or which trends, but if the premises are true, then they do exist. I now recall my vivid discernment in 3 74, when I saw not only Rome around me but the brisk rushing of the Christian underground as they arranged certain things to happen, specifically the fall of the tyranny. I must have seen exactly what

Then it was at least two temporal selves which broke in in 3-74: a past self (Thomas c. AD 70) of a future self (v. Ted Sturgeon's "Circle of Selves" [..]) information was flowing in 2 directions at me, as if 3-74 was a center. The script of my 3-74 life was broken by the future self - or, as Sturgeon says, there a macro self outside space + time, as well as the circle of selves. It is possible though that a future self in space + time knew - from historic records? - What happened to me upon "Tears" coming out, + broke through backwards. But the additional presence of the past self Thomas suggests a fusion of all selves - i.e. the timeless macroself. The macro self took over, + it embraces all the selves.

But how does the externally visible Zebra fit into all this? The elongated time-sense induced flash-cutting [..] of permutation - elongated through all the selves; at that [..] Zebra - the intelligence of the unfolding entelechy (cosmos) [..] visible. But the "still small voice." Zebra's? Or my macro (or future?) self? My future self would know the answer to this. The voice described itself as "tutulary."

Then the dream material in "Tears" was an experiment of sorts, a deliberate attempt by a future self to insert material in writing which to them was the past. Were several alternative futures set up c. 1974? The intervention altered history? The insertion of the material proved it was possible. Thereupon they blitzed the Watergate situation + forced Nixon to turn over the tapes - thus changing history.

1) a future temporal self intervened retroactively in time. or:

2) A (the) timeless macro-entity intervened. or

3) both were present, which explains why Thomas was there, too, from 50 AD Koine. He came with the macro self; [..] was/is my major temporal "injection." (Since, according to my theory, the early Xtians had learned to make up - connect by anamnesis - these several temporal personalities as one, as a supratemporal one: this was what Christ [..] the Holy Spirit taught + enabled them to do. This (Ted Sturgeon's theny) is the superphysics the Xtians were into. This is why he could promise resurrection + eternal life - with him, maybe he is the sole macro entity, of us all, which injects itself into temporal forms - + forgets?

I guess they, although sequential in linear time, occupy the same space as the same orthogonal time; they are all here spacially as well as (from the standpoint of the polytemporal viewer) temporally - all superimposed. I guess the mystery religions - starting with the Elyseans - discloses this. Psychedelics may have been used to break down the temporal compartmentalization. For instance, Thomas, "back" in AD 70, has experienced a life - his life - 2000 years in the (his) future. Him occupying me + trusting me (PKD) aside is just one side of the coin. The other is the experience, knowledge (gnosis) + certitude of immortality which the macro entity, the Holy Spirit, gave him.

I'm going for broke + say, probably all the polytemporal personalities merged in 3-74 to give aid in the [3-75] trap situation. Perhaps this equals the macro entity. I guess in the previous go-around for me (PKD) without this symbiosis. I was done in. + I must not forget the general historic intervention. Future selves were working backward to our time via the macromind, + perhaps pastimes were doing so, also (if this be true then it was perhaps the Parousia, the time of the Presence).

Sturgeon speaks of one being trapped by the power. Perhaps I was trapped (with amnesia) + so needed the help of the other polyselves.

Anyhow, using Sturgeon's model, it is clearly evident why the Gold Fish necklace had the effect on me that it did - given my simultaneous life as an apostolic Xtian of the First Century. Since the Holy Spirit had entered (into symbiosis with) those early, authentic Xtians, "Thomas" could be regarded as the erleben of all the polyselves. Then in a sense the rest of us are forms of him; our route to self knowledge lies through him + what he knows (because of the Holy Spirit decending over him in theolepsy).

If the BIP was from the past, then I bet that the PTG was a future polyself: its world. Just as I was once in the BIP, later (to use linear time terms) I could live a life in the PTG.

All this indicates that I was absolutely right [..] my Metz speech about tracks A, B, + C. What I did not realize is that arranges along the linear-time axis. They are sequential, but (as I said in the speech) they are alternatives in orthogonal time, + when we move from one to another we suffer amnesia re the one or ones we had been inhabiting.

This "track transfer" when viewed as Ted Sturgeon views it coincides with the several worlds of "Tears" which Ted reviewed, + from which (if not also from my previous works) he realized, as he said in the interview I kept bumbling in + out of an understanding of.

It is quite possible that a time (linear) will come in which a scientific knowledge will exists by which the coaxial worlds of "Tears" can be readily understood + explained.

Last edit about 5 years ago by sekennedy62

The other realization I've had is that the BIP underlies all the linear time worlds, + is under attack by set (Zebra). But this attack can't be seen unless several space time continuums are superimposed.

----------

How can it be said that the lives are simultaneous to their time continua are not simultaneous? Can one deny that CE 70 came before CE 1974 which came before, say CE 2019? If they are distributed at points along a linear time line they are consecutive. But Ted Sturgeon is right. I know he is right: I saw CE 70 + CE 1974 superimposed - i.e. simultaneous. I recognized them as such; I knew CE 70 was not memory of a prior or earlier time. But rather a time taking place (like CE 1974) now. Yet CE 70 + CE 1974 are separated by 1904 linear years. How can these facts be reconciled?

It must have to do with the tracking. For example take the spiral groove of a record. Draw a radius running (or course) at right angles to the groove:

[Diagram] A: the groove |A|B|C|D|E|F etc B: a radius drawn |A|B|C|D|E|F etc[/Diagram]

prove that reality is generated by the person; there could be something like a series of "frequencies" of plural space-time continua, into one of which the person is locked, to the exclusion of the others although they are there. The variable condensor of a radio (or channel tuner of a tv set) might be a model to turn to. So even space-time continuum is omnipresent, but the person tracks the groove - ascends the manifold of linear time (the LP groove). We are carefully tuned to a time frequency, + stayed locked to it until death. But if Sturgeon is correct, in some way we simultaneously are also locked into other parts of the groove. There is compartmentalization here. Just as FM station transmissions do not impinge on one another (whereas AM do.).

Again, "Ubik" seems to imply this, re time + reality. There is even theorization in that novel. "Tears," despite the synchronization of time in the various "alternate worlds" may even be closer. Suppose the two novels are combined; we get alternate times + alternate presents.

I had the distinct impression in 3-74 that I had lived through that time span once before, only this time a power or entity from the future had interferred to alter + improve it. This time it came out different, + deliberately so.

The manifestation (intervention, memory) of a future me does not prove Ted Sturgeon's theory of the circle of war correct, but it gives weight to the argument. I can reason that a linear future me intervened backward in time, inserted material in "Tears" etc, but that doesn't explain Thomas or why I found myself in both "my" 1974 world + "his" AD 70 world. So I think Ted is correct. It's as if all my lives got together to handle the 3-74 problem, + that points to the "injecting macro entity."

----------

I have to get perspective on this. The Ted Sturgeon - Scott Apel notice of simultaneous "former" + "later" lives is absolutely the explanantion of why 1) I saw Rome; 2) I believed I was in danger from the Romans; 3) heard the koine; 4) was taken over by "Thomas." Either another of the simultaneous lives broke through to me or I broke through to it. + part of the theory is that the full script of each of the selected lives is known to the macro mind before it injects itself into it.

Then a macro entity injected itself into this life (as well as past + future ones; e.g. Thomas, the 3-eyed evolved humans; etc) +, in symbiosis, as Jeter believes, worked with me, surfacing to take full control in 3-74 (But it was there back when I was in high school, + later too). This macro entity runs a number of lives spread out along the linear time line in sequence. But, for it, simultaneously. Either I was bailed out by the macro entity or by other lives. Past + future Christianity is involved; the macro mind - which I saw extension as Zebra - Christ. In any case, I am one part of either a supratemporal macro intelligence or a poly temporal intelligence. I'm not "the only game in town," the only entry in the race. But it is not PKD who is this. PKD normally only has knowledge of this one "entry" in the race,

22

got not only my memories back (to meet a -the- crisis) but also my total supernatural powers (vis-a-vis the Xerox letter). From before birth I could see -foresee- everything which was going to happen to me. That's why I knew the exact day the Xerox thing was coming, + that my house would be hit on Nov 1971 + that [I'd] meet Tessa that day, etc. Ted Sturgeon's theory, which he had wanted to tell me for 15 years, is basicaly this. + it is basically correct. Each of us leads many lives + they are voluntary, with complete foreknowledge of what it's going to be like. What pleases me is how I laid down the disinhibiting stimulus for 2-74 so as to be able to cope with the Xerox thing, + how I put code unbeknownst to myself in "Tears" - code that will disinhibit others (of myself). It's a lot like "Maze", voluntarily going into a fake world of your own creation + voluntarily shedding your memory so that this fake world can be taken as real. + it's like the "Walt" clue in "Stigmata". So my writing will remind us of the true state of things; it's good to wake ourselves or ourself up. + we will do so where necessary, by such means. "Stigmata" is fascinating when viewed this way: they go voluntarily into a fake world + then forget it's fake, or later, with Eldritch's world, go in voluntarily + can't get back out. "Maze" too. Even "Eye" can be read this way: the forgetting. + "Joint". The Kerygma: (esp. "Maze" + "Stigmata"): We created a fake world, went into it voluntarily + voluntarily shed our memories, + can't get back out, but would like to. However, foreseeing this we providently laid down clues in advance to remind us when the going would get too tough.

Sequence: 1) Created a fake world. 2) Entered it. 3) Forgot our identity (deliberately). 4) Suffered. But then: 5) Came across a deliberately-placed clue which I (we) put there to restore memory when things went too rough - or, more profoundly, to set a limiting factor on this journey of calculated self-deception + imprisonment so it would have to end finally. (Whew - + just in time.)

A spin off theory is that of the creator who will not ask any of his created creatures to undergo what he himself won't undergo. That would otherwise be unjust. This is what he did as Jesus. But to make the [suffering] experience totally that of his creatures, he'd have to block his own memories + not know who he really was, + suppose he was just another mundane created creature. (I'm not supposed to tell this, though. "I am a secret immortal early Xtian" now stands for, "I am deity himself," which I certainly don't want to tell anyone! (Many Sufis believed this, as did Empedocles). First of all, they'd lock me up + throw the key away, + (2) it'd give away the game to some of the other pluriforms prematurely. The game would be aborted - spoiled. But my writing is fine - it provides tantalyzing cryptic clues, not flat out statements, which is good. Just enough, but not the story in clear. "Tat tvam asi"; not to say openly, but rather to speak in parable. When you choose a particular life to go down into (v. Ted Sturgeon) you know in advance exactly what it's going to consist of. Like, I knew about the hit on my house, about Tessa, + esp. about the Xerox letter. I even knew, as the AI voice (myself

The control (Valis) would want our world to move at high speed in relation to it (i.e. to Valis) as in Sturgeon's "Microcosmic God," since it would put vast stretches of change-process under the [scanning] scrutiny + hence power of the control. Suppose the reverse obtained: we were coagulated in relation to Valis. Valis would want to hurry along the linear time strip of our reality to locus after locus rapidly. This is achieved by putting our reality on fast forward, with Valis inserting crucial adjustments at key times + places. If we moved much more slowly than Valis, Valis would die before events it needed to influence ever came about. But I see Valis slowing down our reality at crucial moments of adjustment by synchronizing himself to our high velocity - a bailing in on his part. At high velocity our system discloses certain constants or patterns which Valis can analyze + direct over long periods of our time. __ Solution: if you travel through space very fast objects "move" fast past you; but this is relative. So when you travel fast through time, processes (change) occur hyper fast. Again, Einstein. In a real sense when you move fast through space,

objects are moving fast (in relation to you). So you move fast through time + processes change move hyper fast in relation to you. Hence Valis, the control, is moving at hyper speed through time; as a result, our world speeds up. The temporal axis must be construed as a spacial one, + then the problems vanish. But I still say paradoxically it slows down in relation to time, + as a result change accelerates; time contracts (the opposite of time dilation). In relation to time, not to change. A disparity must be achieved, rather than a synchronization. In Sturgeon's story the microscopic people would see the macro-human moving very slowly, if at all. __ Our world permutates hyper fast in relation to Valis. But neither Valis nor our world is a (or the) constant; here "fast" + "slow" -i.e. faster or slower- are relative to each other, like two moving trains. There is a third thing that is the constant (in my opinion). This would be the Earth in relation to the two trains. But is time transversed, like space, or consumed, like energy? Each -transverse, consume- in relation not to each other -Valis to world- but each to the constant. So if time is space, Valis moves

This is exactly what Ted Sturgeon told me is what happens. This makes me wonder what it was that caused him to want to tell me that. Something in me + my books? Even if the Xtian business is marginal (for example merely having to do with one subsoul - i.e. a particular incarnation -) this business of the master soul deliberately incarnating itself + causing -inducing- anamnesis each time (or perhaps only when the situation gets so dire it is necessary?) could still be the case.
